payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's made out of English brown oak, the center Critical Role inlay is made of ash burl, is the background, and then rosewood for the d20 outline, wenge for the Critical Role words themselves, and silver stringing around the border. It was built by hand in their Massachusetts office and workshop, and it was hand-rubbed oil on a wax finish. Over a hundred hours were put into this table. They did an incredible job, and we're super honored to have that here, and thank you guys for showing such an awesome appreciation for what we do.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll write another one for Easter, I promise. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Thank you, Sam. So thank you, Backblaze. Happy to have you for our sponsor tonight as well. Also, as a heads-up, we've had our friends at Wyrmwood for a while have been awesomely providing Wyrmwood dice boxes and dice trays and dice towers to give away to people in the community. They have new boxes for all the players in the new campaign. And they have been working on something for a while for us to kick off the new campaign. They made us a new table. If we can go to a camera view of this table they made for the campaign. (cheering)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: And final thank-yous to Warner Bros and Monolith Entertainment for letting us incorporate the Shadow of War soundtrack into our Critical Role soundtrack here on the air, so those will be coming into the new campaign. And, super excited, CD Projekt Red has been nice enough to allow us use of the Witcher series soundtrack to also incorporate into the campaign, which, honestly, when we started playing at home, the Witcher soundtrack was most of my music, and I couldn't use it when we started streaming, and so it's been a cool full-circle to be able to bring it back in. Plus it brings a unique flavor that works with the new campaign setting, so. With that, I believe our announcements are done. Thank you for bearing with us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He actually painted me and Marisha's cake toppers for our wedding, and whenever you manage to see them in the campaign, you can know that it's his handiwork. Soon enough.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Also, fun fact about Ian Phillips  I grew up with him. He was one of my brother's best friends when we first moved out here to LA, and so when that name was floating around, I was like, wait a minute. And then we reconnected back on Instagram. It's such a crazy story. You know, Critical Role brings it all back around. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I love it. So you can follow him, @PaintingTusk on Twitter.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I've had him paint 20 to 30-- I'm kind of addicted-- 20 to 30 miniatures. They're amazing. He's amazing at what he does.

payload.raw_text: So Caleb, as you come to consciousness, you glance over and can see, slowly snoring and rousing at about the same time, Nott's eyes blink open, her slowly groaning face looking over towards you.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: All right. As your smaller friend curls awake, Sam, would you like to describe your character?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yeah. Um. I am a little goblin girl. (laughter)

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: I am a goblin. So, you know, the green skin, the green hair, the yellow eyes. And she wears not-great clothes, just like her traveling companion there. She hides in the shadows a lot, because she knows goblins aren't welcome in this part, and that's about it. I mean, she's a little skittish, and right now she's probably stirring awake as well, right?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So Caleb, as you come to consciousness, you glance over and can see, slowly snoring and rousing at about the same time, Nott's eyes blink open, her slowly groaning face looking over towards you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So far you're just talking about current Liam.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What color are your eyes? I need visual.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: They're blue. That's it. It was a rough day yesterday, and-- that's it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And your name is?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Caleb. Caleb Widogast.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. As your smaller friend curls awake, Sam, would you like to describe your character?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Um. I am a little goblin girl. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I am a goblin. So, you know, the green skin, the green hair, the yellow eyes. And she wears not-great clothes, just like her traveling companion there. She hides in the shadows a lot, because she knows goblins aren't welcome in this part, and that's about it. I mean, she's a little skittish, and right now she's probably stirring awake as well, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well. What's your name?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh. Nott the Brave.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: A short time passes before the two platters are rushed over and scattered across the front of your table with a clattering. Pile of meats there set for you, a little bit placed on the side, because you might need, especially with the look on your face-- Adelaine gives you a glance and goes, "Put something in your belly." Comes back and slides two Trosts to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I can get you all three, it's fine. What you want?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: A Trost for each of us, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "For breakfast? I like you. All right, two Trosts and a bunch of meats. Be right back." And she rushes off, she pulls her hair back and ties it as she goes into the kitchen, getting ready for the midst of chaos. While you guys have a moment there at the table, you can hear conversation muttering around you, and you have a brief bit of privacy to discuss.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I need books, that's true, but did you find drink yesterday?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I have a little left in my flask.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How much?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Just a couple swigs.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So that's also on the to-do list, then.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I mean, if you don't want me to get too jittery, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Okay. So we'll do that first.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I love her. (laughter)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As Rinaldo sits down, clutching his hat still in his hands, says, "I thank you for your time. Fjord. And, I apologize, I forget your names."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. And you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Uh, yeah. Yeah. I'm, you know, simple girl, simple needs. Just wearing nice, baggy clothes, some monk vestiges. They're in nice blues and greys. You notice I have a blue sash around my belt. Got a nice shave going on, little undercut. Looks like maybe I put on makeup two days ago, and I'm like, yeah, it's still holding up. It's fine. I can work with that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What's your name?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We'll get to that. Thank you. And Travis, if you'd like to describe yourself, please?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (Texan drawl) Yeah, I'm here too. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm a half-orc, sitting here in beat-up leathers. Have a big scar across my face.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (sings 'Bad to the Bone') (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Green skin tone, and got some piecemeal armor put together and stuff. My name's Fjord.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You're very handsome. I just need to tell you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Thank you. Appreciate that.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: There are four gold pieces amongst the pile, 22 silver, and 48 copper pieces.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, I mean, he did go around to a bunch of people. That was a lot of work on his part. I would hate to make him go back to all of those people and give it back to each individual person.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fine, fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: If he didn't keep a ledger, you know. We wouldn't want to do that. It's rude not to accept a gift, right? It's rude not to accept a gift, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I would hope that is the case. Anyway. Thank you very much. I am sorry for getting in your way." And he stands up and gives you all a nod and rushes out of the tavern, a little embarrassed by all the attention that's now placed on him by the other patrons, and as he leaves, everyone goes back to their business, a couple of wandering eyes starting to count what coins are there as you slowly mitt them in your direction.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Divide it up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, okay.

payload.raw_text: For the next hour.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns_last5.text: Any magic kills a goblin instantly. Yeah, instantly. (laughter)

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: That would be the worst character choice ever. (laughter)

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay, so there's a slight sense of warmth that emanates from under her hand, and for a moment, you look over and glance instinctively, ready to almost draw your sword again. But it seems to quicken your reflexes. You have this surge of adrenaline that doesn't quite fade.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: You have advantage on all your stealth checks.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: For the next hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yesterday was a very bad day.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Well, I suppose if we surrounded ourselves with weirdos, then maybe it would take some of heat off of me.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, yes. Bottom line  can you hang with the goblin?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Bottom line-- and I touch Nott's shoulder, and I cast Blessing of the Trickster.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, Jesus, she's going to kill me right now. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Any magic kills a goblin instantly. Yeah, instantly. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That would be the worst character choice ever. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We will mourn her. Tschuss.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so there's a slight sense of warmth that emanates from under her hand, and for a moment, you look over and glance instinctively, ready to almost draw your sword again. But it seems to quicken your reflexes. You have this surge of adrenaline that doesn't quite fade.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You have advantage on all your stealth checks.

payload.raw_text: But you manage to get nice and sauced throughout the day.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yeah, we can do that. We'll say, if you guys decide to go ahead and travel, the five of you, you make your way through the town for the day, you coast around the Husseldorf family brewery, you meet Voss and June, who are the two very nice ladies who run the facility. The Husseldorf family. You avoid the Baumbauch, but can acquire Baumbauch ale from other taverns in the town, of course. And the von Brandt family brewery. All of them, as soon as you say 'Trost', they correct you and say the family name. They're very intent on getting rid of the name 'Trost' as an overall, discussion of Trost-brand brews, and trying to make it their family name. There's an offense, to the point where you're like, "Oh, Jesus. I didn't realize."

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: Like Kleenex and tissue paper? All right.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 12, okay. You get a little woozy for a while, and as the sun begins to crawl towards the distant horizon, you do not manage to allow your meal to usurp your control.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Like Kleenex and tissue paper? All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But you manage to get nice and sauced throughout the day.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I take it easy. I have one more in the first half of the day, and one in the second half.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't have very much either.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to have tons.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I get sauced.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, well, that being the case--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: --you are poisoned.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, make a constitution saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, that was crazy. 12.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 12. You take the better part of 30 minutes or so, and you find there's a lot of books on farming techniques. There's a lot of books about how to grow, care for different types of vegetation, plants, various beasts of burden. You find books on the different seasons and what's best for different types of grain and wheats. You manage to pull through and find a couple of books that deal with discussion of the Menagerie Coast that talk about what's called the Clovis Concord. It's more of a discussion of the political structure that maintains all the city-states there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: --you are poisoned.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, make a constitution saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, that was crazy. 12.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 12, okay. You get a little woozy for a while, and as the sun begins to crawl towards the distant horizon, you do not manage to allow your meal to usurp your control.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Aw, it's like Ren Faire.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Don't remind me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Good times, good times.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So as the day comes to a close, you're fairly tipsy, you're not fully intoxicated. For your trip, you wanted to head to the Hillsbrook Parchment and Binding.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that where I went the last time? Except not as myself. So I'll go as myself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Go ahead and make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Don't fuck me, Vax. That is--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's Vax. That's Vax's dice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, the thing about even a light German accent is that when you say "Vex" and "Vax", it's the same shit. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is a 12.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Sure, yeah. I'll allow that. All right. Folks begin to prod their heads out into the open air, curious about the source of this unusual din, and you find your vision capturing a small procession of flamboyantly dressed people parading through the central road. A lanky man of some obvious elven descent leads the pack, his long, ashy-brown hair curling ever-so-slightly past his mid-back, his long coat and tails knocking around by his skipping step. Two halfling women in purple and green bodysuits dance from side to side, handing flowers to children and flyers to adults, as a bald man in a neck-frilled frock coat of bright red, his face adorned in vibrant orange makeup to look like the setting sun behind him, plays the violin that you heard earlier in an upbeat, jovial manner. A tall half-orc masculine man with a well-groomed handlebar mustache that curves out to the side, billowing white silk shirt and black trousers, follows behind with a big drum slung over his shoulder, going (drumming) along with the fiddle. The tiefling man of lavender skin that you saw earlier walks along with a grin, juggling two curved scimitars as he walks in place, almost loses one and catches it, continues to go. Families are beginning to gather out to see this display. The two dancers part, and then a woman of short, fiery red hair and dark skin walking between them, lifting a small candle before-- fwoosh! A giant burst, a gout of flame emerges from the front of her mouth, brightens the vicinity, and everyone collectively gasps and begins cheering and clapping as they continue the walk. There, to the back of it, you see the rather burly-looking pale woman, arms crossed, just following behind and keeping an eye like a security guard to ensure that no business gets out of hand. The gasps and the cheers begin to follow, and as the procession continues down its way, the families and civilians begin to gather and follow behind. As the last bit of the sun sets behind the mountain range, the torches glowing, the procession curves through the center of the Loch Ward to the edge of the Ustaloch itself. Through the fishing village, more folks begin to gather. Do you all follow?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I didn't even think about that until now. God damn it. All right, so as you guys have gathered at this point, the oranges and the pinks of dusk begin to peek through the quite-cloudy sky, the chilled air of the coming night sky signalling the Crown's Guard to begin lighting the hanging lanterns that line the streets of Trostenwald. A renewed energy takes the streets as the sound of a fiddle seems to creep through the night air.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: My god, Ashley's a master fiddler, isn't she?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Beedle-deedle. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I love how your fiddle noises were "beedle-deedle".

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: That's the sound for all instruments, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Drums.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: By the way, DM, can I ask-- sometime in the entire day, when Nott was getting wasted, could I have spent an hour using Arcane Recovery to gain back that one spell slot I used this morning?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Sure, yeah. I'll allow that. All right. preceding_turns.text: By the way, DM, can I ask-- sometime in the entire day, when Nott was getting wasted, could I have spent an hour using Arcane Recovery to gain back that one spell slot I used this morning?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Sure, roll an investigation check. I'll give you advantage because you're really good at this.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I promise you I will give you all of your weapons back.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Pinky-swear me, Yasha.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Ugh. I don't like--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! Now we're best friends. Here's my sickle.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I have a staff, but I also use it as a walking stick, so I like to keep it on me.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Can you not walk around without it or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She has a really bad limp.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Sometimes I get a little twinge, a little disc issue.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Can you hand me your staff so I can watch this? I want to see this limp of yours. Purely for my own entertainment.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are you patronizing me, Mollymauk?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, I'm hoping that you're going to patronize us. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I keep my stick? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: How about this? I'll take it, and I can just carry you to your seat.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Deal.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How are you carrying all of these weapons?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Because I'm really fucking strong. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: You. Can I give him a pat-down, because you're--

payload.raw_text: You have advantage on that, technically, right? Dexterity checks?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure, roll an investigation check. I'll give you advantage because you're really good at this.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I open up the coat just so she can see the books.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I don't think I find anything. 12.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You find two books and a modicum of change and nothing else on him. He's actually pretty sparse as far as collectibles and keepsakes.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I just thought your chest looked rather square, so I wanted to see what was happening.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm practically concave. Is it concave or convex, I forget?

preceding_turns.speaker: ALL
preceding_turns.text: Concave.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Convex would be funny. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll hand her my shortsword, but not my little crossbow.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, make a sleight-of-hand check to try and hide that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Four. Oh, wait. 12.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: So, as the music has died down and everyone begins to crowd in and whisper to themselves, eventually it begins to quiet a bit, everyone having their seats. The familiar sound of the fiddle begins to play, and everyone quickly gets quiet, down to a hush. The music seems sourceless in the interior of the tent. Everyone begins to look around to see where it may come from. You hear points of hushed "look"s, pointing off in different areas, and at the top of the tent, you see, seeming to stretch and fall, like a slow drop of water, the man in red you saw earlier descends from the top of the tent, but now dressed head-to-toe in a matching deep blue outfit and full makeup, to where he appears out of the canopy of the tent. He hangs from a rope by one foot, it's wrapped around and clutched as his foot catches the edge. Playing his violin ever-so-slowly as he carefully descends, and then at the last moment releases the rope and lands on his feet, continuing the music he's playing. He slowly spins as the longer-haired elf leader of the earlier parade that you saw, the one that had marked back briefly to your two new friends over here, enters the tent from the distant flaps, the performers' entrance, his head now sporting that same tall hat, flopping backwards at the apex. He extends his arms, removing the hat, and takes a deep bow as the crowd begins to clap. (applause)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's a very swaddling hug. You've never experienced anything like it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's the best way to see the show, if that's how you want it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll give you five silver just to have your shrug to sit on so my butt's not on the floor.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I don't take off my shrug.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: How about you just keep your money and I'll put you in a chair and I'll just watch over your weapons.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So she places you on the ground, as there are no chairs.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: On the ground, I mean, because there's no chairs.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, there's no chairs? Aw. I was picturing benches. That's okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: There's no benches? Oh, fuck.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Where are they going to transport benches from place to place?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: That's a good point.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Carnival? I don't know. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, sorry, you have Fletching and Moondrop to deal with.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Bring a cushion from home like a regular person.

payload.raw_text: "I ask you, each and every one of you--" You hear some children giggle as he makes eye contact. "--grant us your imaginations this eve but a trifle bit of time, and allow us to reveal a realm of laughter, mystery, danger, and beauty. I see you've already met Desmond." And he gestures towards the man in all blue who's playing the fiddle. "He'll be part of our story tonight, so keep a wily eye for the shifting fool. But first, I tell you a tale of two sisters of the Fey." And as Desmond slowly leaves the tent, you see two armored guards by where he is now have entered, two of the Crown's Guard are stepping by the entrance where he walked in, watch him pass. "Lost without form into the mazes of the underworld. There, where the body would break, they found a teacher in a mystical serpent, and the gift they received was the ability to bend with this maze that captured them and slither their way back to the surface to reclaim their place in the world. May I present to you Mona and Yuli, the Knot Sisters." As he slowly backs through the performers' entrance, the two halfling dancers you saw earlier, in the full bodysuits, emerge from behind the Carnival Master as he discreetly exits. They sport serpentine makeup, scales affixed to the sides of their face and arms. Approaching the central spire, the pole that holds the tent up, one immediately leaps onto the shoulders of the other, bending her back a near-impossible amount, creating this humanoid sculpture that you see the front row cringe at the idea of the pain that would cause an average individual. But these halflings are very nimble and able to create almost a single form out of two bodies. Their rigid form becomes fluid to the eye as they move and shift to the music, the violin now sourceless once again, not seeing where this Desmond is placed, but they seem to move as it picks up speed.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: "Ladies and gentlemen of Trostenwald, I am Carnival Master Gustav Fletching, and allow me to welcome you to the Fletching and Moondrop's Traveling Carnival of Curiosities." (applause)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As they both begin to back away, smiles on their faces, nodding to each other, Gustav emerges once more from the performers' flap. The fool appears at the back entrance again, taking an ominous tune with his violin this time, his outfit no longer blue but deepset black. The hanging lights darken once again as Gustav takes the center stage. "In a flash, beyond the ash, the gods all went and gone. The darkness came to grasp, reclaim, and suffocate the dawn." Suddenly, out of the darkness behind him, a burst of flame lights the room. "But from that night, a burning light doth keep back shadows' bane. The strength to fight will set alight the morning sun again. Ladies and gentlemen, I present to you Ornna the fire fairy." And Gustav leaves the tent once more. As you watch, a pair of metallic fans, alight with flame, now frame the beautiful woman of short red hair you had seen before in the procession. Gustav slinks into the darkness and vanishes as Ornna spins in hypnotic twirl, her simple dress of golds and reds almost glowing in the firelight around her, the flickering of the flames giving her the visage of a warrior-goddess battling the dark that surrounds. The frenzied fiddle of the fool picks up as she twirls the fans in her grasp, her face stoic and graceful simultaneously. The hushed awe of the audience fills the air, watching this magnificent warrior strike at the shadows, the lights above flickering brighter with each spin and dive, banishing the shadows from the room. She matches the pace of the music, drawing to a crescendo, and leaps, and barrel-turns and climaxes with her striking a powerful pose as the lights rocket to a victorious luminescence. Once again, the crowd goes crazy. (applause)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Gustav enters as she exits with a bow, the fans flickering out. He enters clapping at Ornna. A low drumbeat begins to beat in the distance as the fiddle grows tense once more. "Even as the sun would rise anew, bellowing roars will quake the lands of Xhorhas and beyond. Terrible beasts, now freed from their dark masters, scattered into our world." (roars) A terrible, guttural roar shakes the room, followed by the sounds of grinding and dragged chains. From behind the performers' flap, the half-orc, Bo, who you'd met earlier, the Breaker, and the fool, who is now still playing the fiddle, yet the bow of it continues to go on its own as he drags the other chain with his arm, wrapped around his forearm. The two of them pull something through the flap. It swells, and from beneath it is revealed an enormous creature of green scales, slimy-looking skin, and a corpulent form, large toad-like legs frame a rotund torso, and muscular arms that are manacled and pulling him in. It fights and wrestles against the taut chains as it's pulled further and further towards the center of the tent, but is held in place by the two men. It roars a second time. (roars) The front row immediately pulling back and screaming. You hear kids begin to cry. A handful of townsfolk shoot to their feet, grasping the children in fear, preparing to run.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: This is really different than most carnivals that I've gone to.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's very artistic, yeah.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Eyes previously locked on the frightening creature now seek a source and find, atop a platform within the chamber, high up on a small platform set against the apex of the pole, a young dwarven girl, maybe 12 years of age, her braided golden hair clasped and her hands at her side, a white dress obscuring her feet. She sings with a mystifying, mature voice that pierces your hearts with joy, unexpectedly and uncontrollably. The crowd audibly gasps as one, the entrancing song of this girl bringing every person into this music. The devil-toad stops struggling against the chains, seemingly caught by the sound of her voice. Its face drops into a soft smile as it slowly walks towards the base of the pole and sits. The half-orc and the fool drop the chains and back away, leaving the beast free in its rapture. Her voice almost begins to summon a chorus from the ether, magical in nature, as there are no others but her joining into this piece. Her smile is infectious, and you can't tear your eyes away. You only now realize the tears down your cheeks, but you cannot break your gaze. From the front row of the audience, on the left, a man stands up, his arms outstretched towards her. His dirtied cloak falls away, his face and skin wracked with age. You see his legs shaking as he stands, his cheeks wet with tears. He shouts (exclaims). His arms drop and clutch his chest. Gurgles a burst of vocal pain once more, and folks nearest to him begin to lean away as he begins to shake. You glance over as the old man yells out once more, as the skin on his arms suddenly begins to tear, the bones splitting from the flesh, writhing, cracking. Dust and blood shake from his wounds as his form swells. Screams begin to ring out from the audience around him, people rising up and running for the exit. Chaos breaks into the room around you. Toya's song abruptly ends, the little girl looking down with fright in her eyes, the devil-toad glancing about worriedly, the half-orc and the fool rushing up beside him to look at the transforming man. The half-orc yells, escorting patrons to the exit, "Everyone! Leave the tent now! Kylre--" As he points to the devil-toad. "--get her to safety." The devil-toad glances up and leaps with a sudden gust of strength to the top and grabs the center pole of the room as it begins to almost quiver to his weight. He grabs the little girl safely under his arm and leaps back down. What are you all doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Have we ever seen anything like this before?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, what's it look like?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, he just described what it looked like.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: By name.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You have not seen this creature. Gustav continues, seemingly unflinched by its approach. "The devil-toad crawls hungrily--"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But what does it look like? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "--into the land of the free folk, lording over nightmares as they say, and what truth lies behind the eyes of this beast? What would be learned when the guiding heart of innocence pierces the hateful soul and brings it to see beauty for the first time? I present to you the vivid voice of Toya." And as Gustav backs away, the devil-toad struggling against the chains, a soft, faint voice begins to slowly emanate from the air-- (a voice appears in the background music) (exclamations)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The old man's body stops quaking, his flesh now grey and mangled like an ancient tree trunk. He turns, his eyes blood-red and bulging, his lips curled into a horrifying grimace. The two Crown's Guard begin to try and make their way through the panicked crowd, but the people, like a wave of chaos, are keeping them at bay. I need everyone to roll initiative. (yelling)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I invoke Duplicity.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You watch as her form suddenly dissipates into multiple seemingly copies of her form as Jester.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yasha? We're going to need those weapons back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I turn to Mollymauk and go, man, I thought it was kind of lame at first, but this is awesome!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Everyone get out right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: This isn't part of the show?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Not part of the show.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's too artsy.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Natural one, no. So the second punch, you swing wide and hit the side of the head, and the neck snaps, and it's now stuck in a sideways position. As it turns, the jaw, it pulls back one of its weirdly swollen clawed hands, ready to go for that open wound in your gut, and as it does, how do you want to do this? (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That'll hit. Damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I had my staff-- oh, I had my staff, but I said--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You said Superman punch, man.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's fine, it's fine. Six damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Six points of damage. Natural 20. You watch as you sock it in the face and the jaw breaks and then snaps back into place. As you land, pulling it back, you can see some of the teeth are actually stuck in your fist as you pull back, they've broken off into your knuckles.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I go, oh, come on. I'm going to use my other ki point to do flurry of blows again, and I'm going to try and punch him in the bridge with the remainder of his teeth.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Her teeth, technically, this is the woman that was killed originally.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. I'll be like, you were so pretty, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go for it. Two more strikes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: 19 and 18. Natural. And then, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's a really pretty dice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Eight, 12, 13 damage.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: (laughs) He goes, "What, over there?" Looks over as you jump over quickly and begin searching through it, and you guys watch her do this, and there's a clear line of sight. As soon as he turns back, he's going to notice her rifling through the other corpse.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What are the corpses doing now that they've fallen?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They're both unmoving, ruined, torn apart.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I search the corpse of the woman?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can. As you go to move to it, both of your forms merge. The Crown's Guard goes like, "Don't move."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Which one?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Both? Uh." He looks genuinely confused.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: When I'm going "which one?" I make my second form just dart forward and start searching the body.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. It mimics searching a body. It's not actually you. Begins rifling imaginary things out of the pockets.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Look at all the stuff that that one's finding over there! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can he turn and look and I try to search the other guy?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a deception check to try and deceive him to glance over.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not bad. 19?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "She's been singing with us for over two years, now."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can he turn and look and I try to search the other guy?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a deception check to try and deceive him to glance over.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Not bad. 19?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) He goes, "What, over there?" Looks over as you jump over quickly and begin searching through it, and you guys watch her do this, and there's a clear line of sight. As soon as he turns back, he's going to notice her rifling through the other corpse.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Is Gustav still in here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Gustav is now walking up, and he's hands up in the air. "I'm terribly sorry. I-- this has never happened. I don't know what happened. What happened?!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We've never seen anything like it before. He just-- he gripped his heart and fell over and turned into that thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It was when Toya started singing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, what's with the dwarven girl? Why--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Nothing like this.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You manage to rifle through the old man's body, through the stringy bits of gore, and you do find three pieces of silver that are just coated in blood. A few moments pass. The rest of the people are gone, and it's just this awkward quiet, and at this point you watch as Ornna, the fire fairy who had previously performed, walks in and is like, "I'm sorry. What has happened here?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We've never seen anything like it before. He just-- he gripped his heart and fell over and turned into that thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It was when Toya started singing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, what's with the dwarven girl? Why--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Nothing like this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "She's been singing with us for over two years, now."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How'd she come to you? She's a pretty young girl.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "She had no family and she was singing in the street for change. There was an opportunity. We seized it. We gave her a family she didn't have."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Put your hands down.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The Crown's Guard goes like (grunts). "It's fine, just don't move."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Did I find anything?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She uses a different dice for everything, Travis?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shut up.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that what I'm dealing with now?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You're not even ready.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He goes, "Don't try that on me. I've been doing this for over ten years."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Have you had the elderly or the sick in these tents before?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you also happen to be very explicit on your page that you don't want the sick, so.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Gustav.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this motion, Gustav goes, "It's generally just because we want to promote a space of welcoming cleanliness. When we're working amongst the outskirts of the Empire, we don't want to bring an element that reminds you of death and--"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You hate old people so much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, we're in entertainment. We have to be cautious." The single Crown's Guard guy who's sitting there with his weapon out, you can see him shaking almost, he's never had to deal with anyting like this before.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Hold up. I've never seen that one before. He looks really suspicious.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Towards him?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, the guy behind him.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps) He does look suspicious.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a deception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sure. Eight.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Ten years two months ago."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yasha will totally kill me. Don't worry, you can leave.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Jester. Jokes are great until they get you killed, man. She's joking.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (weak laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Poor guard.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Immediately rubs his eyes. You can see him, now that he's closer to you, you can see he's sweating, he's scared, he's generally trying the best he can to use whatever training he's received to deal with a scenario he was not trained for.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: This is the King's Guard guy?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: One of the Crown's Guard. The Crown's Guard are all generally hired and trained militia that are sent amongst all the cities to keep the peace.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What's your name, man?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Wow, he's really shook up.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Jamieson.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Flynn."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Flynn. How long you been doing this, you said ten years?
